events.title: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour
events.description:
Set against the iconic backdrop of Sunset Park’s historic waterfront, the massive, revitalized structures of Industry City form a breathtaking stage for an exclusive culinary journey. This premium food and cocktail tour is a curated exploration of taste and craft, transforming an evening into a memorable, high-end experience.
Your adventure begins with artistic bonbons at Lilac Chocolates, explores gourmet imports at Sahadi’s, and includes a refined whiskey tasting at Fort Hamilton Distillery, followed by a sophisticated sake flight. Savor additional artisanal discoveries within the awe-inspiring industrial halls.
Perfect as a distinctive date night, this tour is also fully customizable for private groups and corporate retreats. We specialize in tailoring this engaging experience for team-building and client entertainment, making it an ideal one-day corporate outing. A sophisticated mini-vacation for locals and a profound discovery for visitors.
events.vendor: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour

events.title: The Devil Wears Packaging
events.description: Shift from 2D to 3D in this rapid-prototyping workshop. Using cardboard as our primary medium, we will explore the basics of 3D sculpture and how to adapt rigid forms to the human body. This session focuses on the architecture of the face and head, guiding you to construct a custom-fit mask, crown, wig or sculptural headpiece that transforms your silhouette. This class is a crash course in manipulating rigid planes into fluid, wearable forms. Whether you are building a bold headpiece or a conceptual accessory, you’ll leave with a unique piece of wearable art and a new perspective on “trash.” This short-form workshop is designed to help you think spatially and build quickly, resulting in a one-of-a-kind wearable sculpture.

events.title: Half Step – Grateful Dead Tribute
events.description:
Tickets Here
Industry City and Get It Right Music in association with Murmrr Presents are thrilled to welcome back Half Step – Long Island’s premier Grateful Dead tribute act!
This show will feature two full sets of Half Step!
Event Info
Date: June 7, 2026
Doors: 1:45pm
Set 1: 2:00pm
Set 2: 3:45pm
Tickets: $20 per person in advance/$25 day of show plus fees
Address: Industry City Bandshell Stage, Courtyard 1/2 at Industry City in Brooklyn New York between 36th and 37th street and 2nd and 3rd Avenues.
ENTER 238 36th Street, Brooklyn, NY on your GPS device
The venue is located in an open-air courtyard featuring a full concert sound stage. Fans can get affordable drinks from the venue’s bar and order food from over 17 options inside the adjoining food hall. Concert is rain or shine.
How to get here:
Take the D, N, R lines to 36th street station in Brooklyn and walk west one block to Industry City. 3 stops from Manhattan. 30 minutes from Herald Square.
From Long Island, take the LIRR to Atlantic Terminal then take the D, N or R train to 36th street.
More About Half Step
Half Step is a group of dedicated fans & students of the Grateful Dead, capturing the spirit, improvisation, sound & complexity of Grateful Dead music. Established in 2004 on Long Island, Half Step has been mastering their craft while winning over crowds of veteran Deadheads, newcomers and non-Dead fans alike.
What makes Half Step stand out from the rest?
Like the Grateful Dead themselves, the band is constantly pushing the envelope, growing together as musicians and performers. Only true fans that have experienced and studied the music with an extreme level of dedication and passion can accurately represent the superb body of work that the Grateful Dead have to offer.
When seeing Half Step live, one can clearly see the attention to detail that the band has in putting on a viable Grateful Dead experience. They stay true to the traditional show format and play sets of tunes in a sequence that could likely have been performed by the Dead themselves years ago. The members of Half Step also use similar instruments, amplifiers and effects to further authenticate the Grateful Dead sound.
In the band’s long history, they have undergone and overcome numerous incarnations. With each new member they come closer to refining their approach while consistently raising their standards. Half Step’s current line up is by far the most seasoned and prepared to bring their show to the next level.
As musicians, Half Step has what it takes to present the Dead’s music to even the most critical of fans. Anyone who has experienced the Grateful Dead in one way or another have formed a unique impression of their music. Half Step has the rare privilege to be in the position to share their collective impression with you.

events.title: Sake for Travelers to Japan
events.description:
Planning a trip to Japan? Sake is everywhere – and knowing how to navigate it will transform your travel experience! Join us at the Sake Studies Center at Brooklyn Kura in NYC for an immersive sake class designed specifically for sake-loving travelers headed to Japan. We’ll give you the knowledge, vocabulary, and confidence to order sake like a local, visit breweries off the beaten path, and discover the styles you love most.
Set inside our state-of-the-art NYC sake brewery, this is a uniquely hands-on, fun and interactive learning experience. You’ll tour the brewery, taste sake fresh from the press, and work your way through a guided flight of 5 premium sakes — including selections straight from Japan. Our Sake 101 presentation will cover everything you need to know, and you’ll leave with practical tools you’ll need to understand and order sake the moment you land in Tokyo. We’ll also walk you through the do’s and don’ts of sake etiquette — from how to pour for your table-mates to the customs and rituals that show respect and make the best impression wherever in Japan your travels may take you.
What’s included:
Insider tips on how to find and visit sake breweries in Japan
A vocabulary cheat sheet so you can confidently order the sake that suits you perfectly
Sake etiquette and cultural customs to help you drink with confidence and respect
Sake 101 presentation covering all the sake basics you need to know
Guided tasting of 5 premium sakes, including selections from Japan
Guided tour through our NYC sake brewery
Sake 101 Handouts to take with you on your travels
Light nibbles to pair with your sakes

events.title: Gay Poets of the 60’s & 70’s
events.description:
Before Pride became a hashtag, a campaign, or a corporate slogan, there were poets turning isolation into identity and pain into culture.
They weren’t chasing algorithms. They “became” the signal.
On June 9th, the Isaac Brown Healing Arts & Community Center presents “Gay Poets of the ’60s & ’70s,” a community experience facilitated by James Wright exploring the voices that shifted conversations, challenged systems, and created cultural impact long before visibility was profitable.
This gathering highlights legendary writers like Langston Hughes, Mae Cowdery, Miguel Piñero, Richard Bruce Nugent, Alice Walker, and other groundbreaking LGBTQ+ voices whose words created connection, representation, and movements that still influence art, music, mental health, fashion, activism, and culture today.
In a world built on attention, these poets understood something early:
stories move people faster than institutions do.
Expect poetry, dialogue, reflection, history, and real community conversation centered around expression, identity, resilience, and creative freedom.
This isn’t just nostalgia.
It’s cultural context.
It’s reclaiming voices that helped build the blueprint for authenticity before authenticity became branding.
Doors Open: 11 AM
Program Begins: 1 PM
Facilitated by James Wright
Free • Person-Centered • Community-Based • All Welcome
At the Isaac Brown Healing Arts & Community Center, we believe healing grows where creativity, visibility, and human connection intersect.

events.title: Community Clubbing Party
events.description:
COMMUNITY CLUBBING isn’t just another event.
It’s a response to a world where people are more connected online than ever, yet more isolated in real life.
Too many people are surviving silently.
Too many people are healing alone.
Too many wellness spaces feel cold, clinical, expensive, performative, or impossible to relate to.
So we decided to do something different.
On June 11th, the Isaac Brown Healing Arts and Community Center is transforming support into culture through music, movement, conversation, creativity, community resources, mocktails, healthy bites, and unapologetic self-expression at our LGBTIQA+ Pride COMMUNITY CLUBBING experience.
This is for the outsiders.
The creatives.
The healers.
The overthinkers.
The music lovers.
The people rebuilding themselves quietly.
The people looking for community without judgment, pressure, or pretense.
Whether you come to dance, network, decompress, laugh, celebrate Pride, meet new people, or simply exist freely in a room where you feel seen this space was built for you.
Expect:
• Live DJ energy
• Community connection
• Wellness resources without the clinical feeling
• Music, rhythm, and movement
• Real conversations
• A room full of people choosing connection over isolation
At the Isaac Brown Healing Arts and Community Center, we believe healing doesn’t only happen in offices. Sometimes healing starts in community. Sometimes it starts in laughter. Sometimes it starts on a dance floor surrounded by people who remind you that you are not alone.
COMMUNITY CLUBBING is built around a simple idea:
What if support felt more human?
Because culture moves people faster than brochures ever will.
Bring someone who needs community.
Send to someone who deserves a space to exhale.
Come as you are.
June 11, 2026
11AM–4PM
Isaac Brown Healing Arts and Community Center
882 3rd Ave, Brooklyn, NY 11232

events.title: Sake and Cheese Pairing Class and Brewery Tour
events.description:
Step away from that bottle of vino and join us to sample the absolute best beverage to pair with cheese – We are talking about sake! If you haven’t yet tried pairing craft sake and premium cheese, you are in for a revelation. We’ll explore a wide range of flavors and textures… and together, we’ll discover the most exciting combinations of sake styles and cheeses from around the world. The experience is fun, interactive and absolutely delicious! Join us at Brooklyn Kura to learn about sake where sake is made!
What’s included:
A welcome sake to get you started!
A brief intro to sake and cheese basics.
Guided Tasting of 5 Japanese craft sakes from different regions paired to perfection with 5 outstanding cheese varieties.
Exploration of sake and cheese production styles and terrior.
Enjoy some nibbles and spreads on your cheese board that will take the pairing over the top.
Handouts so you can bring your newfound sake and cheese knowledge home
Finish with a Guided tour through our new Sake Brewery
events.vendor: Sake and Cheese Pairing Class and Brewery Tour

events.title: VinylCon! NYC
events.description:
A community celebration of collecting culture and discovering new music. A music collector’s daydream come to life, VinylCon! is a two-day community celebration of collecting culture and new music discovery.
We bring in individual vinyl sellers from all over the region and beyond to set up a massive music selection you can’t find anywhere else. Many sellers from outside of NYC.
Everything from collectors looking to clear out shelf space to professional shops and dealers.
We are focused on vinyl but don’t worry there will be other recorded media on offer, as well as all sorts of music memorabilia and ephemera.
To round it out we will have a full bar, exciting sponsor activations, and of course we’ll be bringing some vinyl-only DJ’s to provide killer tunes while you dig!

events.title: Family Printmaking Lab
events.description:
This relief printmaking workshop introduces participants to image-making using styrofoam plates as an accessible alternative to traditional relief blocks. Intended to be done with both parent and child, this workshop will open new possibilities for expression for both children and adults. Through an intuitive, hands-on process, carve a unique drawing into a styrofoam block and explore how pressure, line quality, and repetition shape the final image to create a small edition of expressive relief prints.
*Pricing per Family

events.title: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting
events.description:
Join us for a tour of our state-of-the-art sake brewery in Industry City, Brooklyn. See first hand how we turn rice and water into delicious Brooklyn-made premium craft sake. Led by our expert brewery staff, this experience will walk you through our production facility and allow you to take in the sights, sounds and delicious smells of New York’s premier sake brewery. The tour concludes in our taproom where we will lead you a guided tasting flight of 3 kinds of Brooklyn Kura and introduce you to the nuance and variety of our award-winning sake.
Location:
Please arrive promptly in our taproom to start your tour. The location is:
34 34th Street, Inner courtyard
Industry City Building 6
Brooklyn, NY 11232
events.vendor: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting
